admissibility.summary: Ideal-membership certificates whose witness identity f = sum_i q_i * g_i is checked by MathEvidence. External systems may search for multipliers, but their basis algorithms are not trusted by this capability.
requiredAssumptions: Certificate contains one sparse integer-polynomial multiplier for each generator
Lean checkMembership remains authoritative for the witness identity
Search completeness and Groebner-basis correctness remain backend-owned and untrusted

knownLimitations: MathEvidence checks only the witness identity f = sum_i q_i * g_i over sparse integer polynomials.
No Groebner-basis correctness, non-membership, radical-membership, ideal-equality, completeness, or optimality claim is made.
SymPy is the always-on witness proposer (Agent compute + benchmark harness).
Mathematica live witness generation requires MATHEVIDENCE_WOLFRAMSCRIPT; not advertised as live when unset (adapter + fixture conformance only).
Sage live witness generation requires sage/sagemath on PATH; not advertised when absent.
Univariate Mathlib Ideal.span Meta auto-bridge closes ordinary singleton and two-generator span goals; MvPolynomial Meta reification remains open (IR + CommRing pair theorem present).
Committed Mathematica/Sage offline fixtures share ≥2 requests accepted by checkMembership; live dual-backend differential blocked without wolframscript/sage on host.

supportClaims.installedHint: python -m adapters.sympy (always); Mathematica/Sage offline fixtures committed; live only when MATHEVIDENCE_WOLFRAMSCRIPT / sage on PATH (not advertised when absent)
